channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 2 hours.

item.title: 🏛️ Army requests funds to speed development, production of EW systems | Breaking Defense
item.description: The Army’s FY27 budget request allows faster fielding and continued experimentation on key electronic warfare and signals intelligence capabilities.

item.title: 📍 Pioneering Plunder: How to Stop Modern Profiteering in Public Office | Just Security
item.description: An examination of Trump-era profiteering and a bold proposal to ban presidential self-enrichment, arguing systemic reform is needed to curb corruption and rebuild trust. The post Pioneering Plunder: How to Stop Modern Profiteering in Public Office appeared first on Just…

item.title: 🌍 An Ayatollah for the Aggrieved | Foreign Policy
item.description: For Iran’s Sunni admirers, resistance remains the appeal.

item.title: 📍 The Oral Argument in Cisco | Just Security
item.description: SCOTUS oral arguments on aiding and abetting liability for US companies that facilitate atrocities abroad highlighted cross-cutting legal views amongst the Justices The post The Oral Argument in Cisco appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 🌍 Early Edition: April 30, 2026 | Just Security
item.description: Signup to receive the Early Edition in your inbox here. A curated weekday guide to major news and developments over the last 24 hours. Here’s today’s news: IRAN WAR – CEASEFIRE U.S. Central Command has prepared a plan for a “short and powerful” wave of strikes on Iran in hopes…

item.title: 🏴 Investigation: Russian shadow airlines use Algeria as base for secretive missions | Defense News
item.description: Algeria has become a key customer for Russian arms at a time when Moscow has seen a slump in its military-export business after the invasion of Ukraine.

item.title: 📍 Protecting Civilians in Good Faith: A Joint Symposium on the Updated ICRC Commentary on the Fourth Geneva Convention | Just Security
item.description: Expert authors examine key interpretive issues and selected topics in the updated Commentary on the Fourth Geneva Convention​ on protection of civilians during civil war. The post Protecting Civilians in Good Faith: A Joint Symposium on the Updated ICRC Commentary on the Fourth…

item.title: 🌍 The Eulogy That Explains Israel’s Current Tragedy | Foreign Policy
item.description: Israel has forgotten the wisdom in an iconic funeral oration delivered 70 years ago.

item.title: 🔐 Correcting Course in the Indo‑Pacific | War on the Rocks
item.description: Last month, President Donald Trump hosted Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi for a visit that captured both the promise and the shortcomings of America’s Indo‑Pacific strategy.In some ways, the meeting was a success: It produced a few commercial deals, reaffirmed the…

item.title: 🕵️ Rethinking Corporate Risk and Alignment in an Era of Economic Statecraft | War on the Rocks
item.description: Editor’s note: This article is the eighth in an 11-part series examining how the United States should organize, lead, and integrate economic statecraft into strategy, defense practice, and the broader national security ecosystem. This special series is brought to you by the…

item.title: 🌏 Expanding frontiers: how China’s outward push could cause friction | ASPI Strategist
item.description: China’s expanding presence beyond the first island chain is most likely to generate incidents, friction and escalation risks not through deliberate aggression but through growing operational proximity. As Chinese military, paramilitary and law-enforcement activity becomes ...

item.title: 📍 For What AI Could Do to Democracies, Look to the Petrostates | Foreign Policy
item.description: Societies will become richer, but history suggests that wealth may not be equally distributed.

item.title: 📍 The New Resource Curse | Foreign Affairs
item.description: Critical minerals will scramble geopolitics.

item.title: 🌏 What Drove Down America’s Fentanyl Deaths? | Foreign Affairs
item.description: China might have—but wouldn’t want Washington to know.

item.title: 📍 US Court Dismisses Rohingya Hate Speech Lawsuit Against Meta | The Diplomat
item.description: Plaintiffs argued that Facebook helped spread hate speech that “amounted to a substantial cause, and eventual perpetuation of, the Rohingya genocide.”

item.title: 🌏 The Quad should share intel on critical-mineral supply risks | ASPI Strategist
item.description: With China increasing restrictions on critical minerals trade, including tighter export controls on rare earth elements announced in March, the United States and allied countries are trying to bring more mineral supply to market. To ...

item.title: 🌍 Israel Now Using Netting To Protect Combat Vehicles Against Scourge Of Hezbollah Drones | The War Zone
item.description: The netting appears as the IDF takes heat for not doing more to defend against FPV drone attacks, although options remain very limited. The post Israel Now Using Netting To Protect Combat Vehicles Against Scourge Of Hezbollah Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy to extend service life of amphibious assault ship USS Wasp by 5 years | Defense News
item.description: The service completed a study of Wasp-class amphibious assault ships and is currently conducting another on amphibious dock landing ships.

item.title: 🏛️ Confidence In CH-53K King Stallion Grows Ahead Of First Operational Deployment | The War Zone
item.description: Going to sea with the 26th Marine Expeditionary Unit is a prelude to the CH-53K hitting full-rate production, planned for FY 2029. The post Confidence In CH-53K King Stallion Grows Ahead Of First Operational Deployment appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ Key takeaways from Hegseth’s day in front of the House | Breaking Defense
item.description: What to know about Pete Hegseth’s appearance in front of the House Armed Services Committee.

item.title: 🌍 Where Is Iran’s Enriched Uranium? | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.N. nuclear watchdog suspects that roughly half of Tehran’s stockpile is still at its Isfahan facility.

item.title: 🏛️ ‘Resign Immediately’: Democrats Grill Hegseth on Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: Congressional Democrats fought with the U.S. defense secretary over the war and military spending in a rare public hearing.

item.title: 🌏 A blueprint for a US-South Korea combined multi-domain task force | Atlantic Council
item.description: A combined multi-domain task force would fill a critical gap in the US-ROK alliance’s deterrence and rapid-response capabilities. The post A blueprint for a US-South Korea combined multi-domain task force appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🏛️ Marine Corps considering Army’s MV-75 as an attack helo replacement | Defense One
item.description: Bell unveiled an armed version specifically for the Corps at the Modern Day Marine conference.

item.title: 📍 India Rethinks Energy Security Amid War | Foreign Policy
item.description: As New Delhi feels the crunch, diplomacy in Abu Dhabi hints at expanding green cooperation.

item.title: 🌍 Don’t call it a ‘quagmire’: defense secretary refuses to speculate on length of Iran war | Defense News
item.description: The questions over the war’s future come as a 60-day deadline approaches Thursday under the War Powers Act.

item.title: 📍 Where Is Pakistan Again? | Foreign Policy
item.description: Why the World Bank’s quiet decision to move the South Asian country to its Middle East column is more than just a bureaucratic footnote.

item.title: 🏴 Trump says he discussed a Ukraine ceasefire with Putin | Defense News
item.description: Trump said he suggested "a little bit of a ceasefire" in the war in Ukraine during his phone call Wednesday with the Russian leader.

item.title: 🕵️ Australia’s innovation funds are multiplying, but the mission still isn’t clear | ASPI Strategist
item.description: When submissions to the government’s request for expressions of interest (REOI) for the Advanced Capability Investment (ACI) fund close this afternoon, officials will face a familiar challenge: not a shortage of proposals, but uncertainty about ...

item.title: 📍 Space Force awards first contracts for satellite threat warning radar payloads | Breaking Defense
item.description: Space RCO is already looking to start a follow-on effort to build smaller threat warning sensors for a wider set of Space Force birds.
